A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Episode 600: Tell Me One Thing That’s Working

March 20, 2019 by Ivan Misner 55 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Congratulations to Ivan and Priscilla on 600 episodes of the Official BNI Podcast!

Sometimes a BNI chapter can get so obsessed with what isn’t working that they lose sight of what is working. This is especially true when the chapter is struggling.

If you’re on the leadership team and your chapter is struggling, ask every member to tell you one thing that is working for them. Write the answers on a whiteboard or flip chart.

Almost every time Dr. Misner asked this question in a chapter, he got answers like these.

  • I’m building great relationships.
  • It’s brought out the best in me.
  • I’m a better speaker than I was before BNI.
  • It’s increased my knowledge of other people’s businesses.
  • I’ve learned how to listen more proactively.
  • It gives me a support group.
  • It helps me set marketing goals.

When you conduct this exercise, every member in the chapter can see that BNI works in many ways beyond referrals. Encourage members to talk to each other about what’s working for them.

The more parts of the BNI program your chapter has working, the better the chapter will be. If you focus on problems, you become an expert on problems. If you focus on solutions, you become an expert on solutions. (Check out Mark McKergow’s book The Solutions Focus for more on this topic.)

Share one thing that’s working for you in the comments below.

For more of Ivan’s content, go to IvanMisner.com.

Priscilla:
Hello everybody and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast which is brought to you by The Networking For Success Channel on YouTube, which features Dr. Ivan Misner and many other networking experts. I’m Priscilla Rice and I’m coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, CA. I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan! How are you and did you know this is our 600th podcast?

Ivan:
I just heard you say that. Episode 600. That is really exciting. We have been doing this for more than a decade. Well over a decade now. I have to be honest. When I did the first podcast I wasn’t completely certain that this would work, that you know, doing a weekly podcast would be something that people are interested in. Now we have tens of thousands of listeners every week. It is really an honor to be doing this podcast with you for so long, Priscilla. 

Priscilla: 
Thank you, Ivan. Well, I feel exactly the same, so that’s great.

Ivan:
So this is a fun topic and I thought I would pull out of my vault a topic that I have talked about for many years when I went to individual chapters and did presentations at individual chapters. I called it the “Is it working?” Presentation but as we go through this, I’ll tell you why we changed the title. Because I am suggesting that you start with “tell me one thing that is working” rather “than is it working?” Let me give you the history to this.

I would visit chapters and I would see chapters so obsessed with what isn’t working that they couldn’t think about what was working. One of the best ways to do a mindset reset – this is sort of organizational behavior 101 – is to start with the most frustration and lead to where there can be success in a discussion. 

I used to go to chapters many years ago, and I would just stand up and ask the chapter – particularly chapters that were struggling. I would say to the chapter, I would shrug my shoulders and say, “Let me ask you a question. Is it working?” And they would kind of look at me for a moment. I would say, “It’s okay. You can be honest. Is it working?” Inevitably, someone would say, “No. No. It’s just not working.”

I would say, “Okay, Great. Thank you for being honest. So do me a favor,” and I always had a flip chart with me when I did this exercise.  I am going to recommend that if you are listening to this podcast, especially if you are on the Leadership Team or Education Coordinator, I would urge you to try this exercise, but try it starting with this phrase. Don’t ask “Is it working?”  Because it is scary and you don’t know how- I dealt with it so many times I knew how to respond to it. I recommend you start with this question: “Tell me one thing about BNI that is working for you. Tell me one thing. I am going to go around the room and I would like you to share one thing that is working.”

Now, when I did this exercise, I started with, “Is it working?“ I don’t recommend you, the listener, do that. Imagine starting with “Is it working?” and having someone say, “No,” and then going to this question and – I would say it to that one and start with that one person who said no. I said, “Fair enough. Can you just share with me one thing that you believe is working for you?” And they would give it. It often was something like “Relationships. I am building great relationships with other quality business professionals. That is working for me.”

Great. And I would write it on the flip chart. Then I would say.to someone else now, “Give me something that is working for you” and I would get this amazing list. I wrote this down years ago. Here are the kinds of things I would get: “Referrals. I am getting business.“ “It has brought out the best in me.” Sometimes people would say, “It has brought out the best. I am a better business person because of this.” Here is one I got almost every time: “My presentations have gotten better. I am a better speaker than I was before I started BNI.” Man, I heard that one, in particular, a lot. “It has increased my knowledge of other people’s businesses.”

Now, when you do this and you say – I am suggesting that if you are listening to this podcast and you are in a leadership role, you ask the question, “Tell me. One thing that is working.” and then write each of these down on a flip chart. You will hear things like, “It has increased my knowledge of other people’s businesses.” “It is like having 30 business consultants working for me.” “I have made great connections in the community.” “I have improved my business skills.” “I have learned how to listen more proactively.” These are actual comments that I would get, Priscilla. “My meeting day – it’s the best day of the week.”  I love that one and I heard it more than once. “This day of the week is the best day of the week for me because I start my morning out in a real positive way.” Here is another one: “It helps me set marketing goals.”  “It gives me a support group to get feedback from.” 

So I would write this list down, and by the way, every time I would do this, “Is it working?” and I would ask them to give me one thing, I would get almost all of these, if not all of them. And then I would ask the group, “This is more than just referrals, don’t you think?”

There are a lot of benefits to being in BNI. So don’t do “It is working?” but say “Tell me one thing that is working.” And watch as the chapter gives you a list very much like this. Then point out to the chapter that BNI works and it works in many ways, including referrals but not exclusive to everything else. Then I would end the discussion by saying, “If you picked one or two or three of these and you think these are working for you, talk to some of the other people about why the other elements are working for them. Because I am here to tell you, that if you as a chapter, were able to get for each and every member the majority of the things that I just asked about – building relationships, getting referrals, presentations getting better, more connections, increased business skills, better listener, best day of the week, a good start for my business. If more and more of your chapter members are getting the majority of these, your chapter will rock the house. Your chapter would be one of the best chapters in the organization.

What was amazing was when I did this presentation, – and I did the “Is it working?” which I don’t recommend you do, because it is a little bit like stepping off a cliff when you do that. It is a little scary. But when I did this, 100 percent of the time the chapter was like, “Yeah, okay, I never thought of it from that perspective, and from that perspective, it is working because all of us are getting so many different things in addition to referrals.”

Now, referrals are important and I talk a lot about referrals in my podcasts, but I think it was time – and this is perfect for the 600th episode of BNI to tell me one thing that is working. So I would love for you if you are listening to this podcast, to do two things: One, I have already suggested it. Do this at your chapter, “Tell me one thing that is working.” Get your own list.
And Two, I would love for you to share with me right now, in the comments section, one thing that is working for you in BNI. One thing that is working for you in BNI.

I have one or two other comments to close, but Priscilla, what do you think of this concept?

Priscilla:
You know, I have always felt that way about BNI because I have kind of an unusual business. I don’t get a ton of referrals but I do benefit from all those other ways. Being part of that business community, learning all of those skills and listening to everybody’s information every week, and also having the resources in the group that I can then refer to and recommend to my other networks, I think, is so valuable. But do you ever ask them what isn’t working or is that too dangerous?

Ivan:
You know what, listen, when I went to visit the chapters – now I do regional events, but when I visited chapters, I didn’t have to ask the question. When people weren’t happy, they were telling me. They were telling me. Here is the problem with just focusing on the challenges:  If all you focus on are problems, you become an expert at problems. but if you focus on solutions, you become an expert at solutions. I know I have recommended this book in the past on BNI Podcast, but it is called “Solutions Focus” by Dr. Mark McKergow. It is probably the best book I have ever read on why it is important to understand what your challenges are.

You can’t hide from challenges. That is a bad idea. But if all you do is obsess over the problems, you just become a really fantastic expert at problems. What I am afraid I see people do in life – not just in BNI but in life in general – is they obsess over the problems and they don’t focus on solutions. So when I go into a chapter, I know there are problems. There are always problems. Even the best chapters in the world have problems. The best businesses in the world have problems. I know they know what those are but what I am looking to do is to do a mindset reset, like I started the podcast with, to reset the mindset to focus not just on the problems but the solutions to the problems. The best way to start focusing on the solutions is to look at what is working. When you know what is working- this is straight out of the “Solutions Focus” book. When you know what is working, then focus more on those things. When you are focusing on those things, it’s magic. The problems dissipate. 

I urge you to read that book and I urge you to try this out, but I urge you to try this out saying, “Tell me one thing that is working.” Create the list and get everyone to focus on as much as possible, everyone improving on those things. Whatever problems you have, I promise you, you will have less of them by focusing on the solutions. That is it for today, Priscilla. 

Priscilla:
That was great. Okay, thank you so much. Good information. For more of Ivan’s content, go to IvanMisner.com. Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Posted in Building Better BNI Chapters, Leadership Teams

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. James Wilson says

    March 20, 2019 at 7:28 am

    BNI University. Having the opportunity to learn on an individual level to bring the highest possible value to not only my chapter referral partners but also to my global team members. As a member who chooses to press in to all that BNI has to offer, BNIU has helped me to cultivate relationships from around the globe. When I first was first introduced to BNIU last year, I thought it would not be possible to reach the Global Leaderboard – EVER. My first action item (after the Member Success Program) was to reach out to Luc Lavergne in Canada (at that time #1) and I scheduled a 1:1 with him. An hour and a half later, I had gained the understanding of what it took for him to obtain his goals on BNIU as well as how similar all of our chapters are around the world (well, perhaps not our friends in India, they are simply AWESOME!) My point is that Luc was able to provide insight from a different perspective on chapter challenges that we many of us must deal with at some time. BNI University is amazing on so many levels and I could speak about it for hours on end as this is how passionate I am about what you and the GST have created. The relationships made possible through exposure to this platform have helped me to become a better leader, communicator and connector. I would like to acknowledge those in BNI who have had a profound & positive impact in my life because of BNIU: Barbara Barre, Phil Berg, Meagan Chitwood, Lynn Raven Fahey, Luc Lavergne, Nitin Munot, Robert Murphy (my DC in Kansas), Tim Roberts (Power of One Podcasts), Jeremy Walsh, YP Lai and Darryl Young. Thank you Dr. Ivan for this platform as you have had a profound impact on my BNI, professional and personal life beyond anything I could have imagined. Kind Regards, James Wilson, BNI Derby Dream Team, Derby, Kansas, USA

    Reply

    March 20, 2019 at 8:54 am

    I am new to BNI only 6 months, I want to say it is teaching Me to treat my business as a business and not a hobby. I am learning so many skills from podcast. I am bring pushed out of my comfort zone which will help me grow my business
    Referrals are coming slowly as the members gain my trust
    I am so happy to be surrounded by serious business minded people

    I get accountable, every week, with my peers for what I’m doing to build my business. I love it! I was able to get a business book written over the past 6 years and I doubt I could have done that without meeting all the people at BNI. It was such an amazing journey filled with accountability.

    Thank you for this podcast. I’m a new potential member going through the process of joining now. I have been listening to the older episodes. But this episodes focus on relationships and improving bussiness sense and other benefits of BNI made me feel better about it.

    Regarding episode #600, The One Thing That Is Working For Me In BNI. I love following the system on BNI Connect. It really works for me and keeps me accountable.     One of the many things that is working for me in BNI is that my business coaching skills have improved thanks to my experience as a Director Consultant.     It is really more than referrals.

    Llenar de entusiasmo a tu grupo con comentarios positivo logra que todos se compromentan a trabajar en equipo y que se contagie las muchas razones por las que pertenecer a un grupo en BNI.
    Además si los visitantes ven como plus estas otras ventajas, además de obtener referencias, podrán ver el crecimiento personal que obtienes al pertenecer a BNI¡¡¡

  11. Yateesh Kukreti says

    March 21, 2019 at 2:03 pm

    Thank you Ivan.

    The insight of this podcast were of great quality and will serve as inputs for me as a start-up organization.

    Solutions to problems the key is my takeaway from it as a forward thinker.

    I Look forward to such motivational advise from you and BNI family in future as well.

    I have already have forwarded the link to this podcast to my chapter Education coordinator.
    She would for sure click the reset note for many of the negatives and turn them into positives for the complete inclusive growth of chapter members.

    Thank you.
    Waiting for #601.

  12. Angela Farrey says

    March 21, 2019 at 2:11 pm

    Liked this so true my meeting boosts me for the week and makes me find refferals for my group

    Reply

  13. Link Wachler says

    March 21, 2019 at 2:36 pm

    Hi Ivan, there are so many things I am getting out of BNI, but other than business, it’s definately having control of speaking effectively and storytelling. I am an introvert and I just did a live Fox 2 Detroit Morning Show for 3 1/2 minutes with a local media host, Charlie Langton. Charlie loves to talk and I had to make sure to stay focused and get my story across in a short time about how I help people, who have lost loved ones and have had their jewelry left to them, redesign and repurpose it to give them healing and to help them honor their loved one’s legacy. I’d love to send you the link (no pun intended) if you’d like to watch. Speaking and staying focused through my 60 seconds really prepared me for this. I’ve been getting business from people who saw the segment and lots of kind and encouraging words about the interview. I’m becoming known in the area as the specialist in “enhancing your family jewels!” Happy 600th podcast. These are the best.

    Reply

  14. Glenda Paton says

    March 21, 2019 at 5:04 pm

    Although a BNI member for many years, I am a new member in a relatively new chapter in a small town in NSW Australia. In my first year, I can say it is working for me in all areas – I am getting referrals and increasing my workload, simply because of the relationships built within this new chapter.

    The one thing is a broader view of our community

    Felicidades por el podcast número 600, y lo que dice el Dr Misner es totalmente cierto hay que enfocarse en cómo te ha ayudado a ti y vendrán las respuestas personales positivas.

  28. Sally Onesty says

    March 24, 2019 at 8:20 pm

    I’ve built life long friendships over 19 years of being a member.

  29. Mali List Mayer says

    March 25, 2019 at 11:09 am

    One thing that is working for me is that I have gotten better at talking about my business and what I do.

  30. Jason Stubbs says

    March 25, 2019 at 1:11 pm

    The big benefit that I get from BNI is the Business acumen that I have learned from the other members. Dont get me wrong we get a lot of referrals, but with our business we are always busy, but how to operate a business successfully has been priceless for me.

    Reply

  31. Debbie Damesworth says

    March 26, 2019 at 7:29 am

    This was great confirmation that we are to look at the positive to find solutions. We are to be grateful for what we have instead of always upset by what we don’t have. I have been in the same BNI chapter for 13 years. It has helped me to be a better leader, to not be shy to stand up and talk in front of others, it has tripled my business, and built precious friends and relationships. Our chapter is going through a difficult time right now, but this Podcast put everything back into prospective and so I want to share it with them. Thanks so much.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 26, 2019 at 3:34 pm

      Yes Debbie, share widely! The average business person focuses mostly on the problems in a situation. The above average business person focuses on the solutions.

      I understand your group has challenges. Just know that I’ve seen groups go from 16 members to 40 members in the same year (and what a HUGE difference that made in their referrals). Here are two other podcasts that I’d share with your group. They might help in that journey.

      https://www.bnipodcast.com/2016/08/10/3-plus-1-member-success-classic-podcast/

      https://www.bnipodcast.com/2017/06/28/creating-a-great-chapter-culture/

      Good luck.

      Ivan

      Reply

    March 26, 2019 at 8:48 pm

    I love being an accountable member of a supportive, proactive, energetic team. And…it’s great to feel like I’m learning and stretching.

    Reply

  33. Ulysses says

    March 27, 2019 at 8:37 pm

    BNI motivates me to grow personally and professionally.

    Reply

  34. Kevin Anderton says

    March 29, 2019 at 11:28 am

    One thing that is working: Not stop referrals. As a graphic designer, BNI has been amazing for me. My group passes me at least one solid referral every week.

    One thing that is not working: Some businesses seem to struggle with making BNI work and I am not sure how to help them.

  35. Julio says

    March 30, 2019 at 10:25 pm

    I asked a Guatemalan first chapter and soñé Members said as follows
    Que cosa ha funcionado para ti en BNI
    1. Oportunidades. Opportunity
    2. Contactos. Contacts
    3. Colaboración. Collaborattion
    4. Networking Estrategico. Strategic Networking
    5. Conocer otros giros de negocios. Knowing other business
    6. Negocios. Business
    7. Contactos
    8. Facilidad de acercamiento a clientes. Open doors for new customer
    9. Oportunidad de Crecimiento. Oportunity of increase business
    10. Apertura comercial.
    11. Compartir experiencias profesionales. Sharing professionals experiences
    12. Networking
    13. Amigos. Friend
    14. Mejorar habilidades de networking. Improve networking skills
    15. Rentabilidad. Rentability
    16. Negocios business
    Julio Lemus BNI Guatemala Director

    Reply

    April 3, 2019 at 6:54 am

    It helps me set marketing goals and focus on the target .

    Reply

  37. Preetha Balakrishnan says

    April 3, 2019 at 7:53 am

    One thing that is working for me is it makes me approach getting referrals in a structured manner. Don’t have to think every morning, whom should I talk, where to go and what to say. It prepares you and helps you to talk to your chapter members to get business. Thanks…

    Reply

  38. Kirsten Fryer says

    April 10, 2019 at 4:47 am

    BNI has helped build my self confidence. Being in a supportive chapter and safe environment, has allowed me to feel more secure and comfortable in business. Thank you!

    Reply

  39. Dhanashree Gandhare says

    April 15, 2019 at 11:54 am

    Hello, one thing that working for me is that its an business institution for me. Being from a working class family and into first generation business I got to know the basics of any business from my other fellow BNI members who are running second third generation businesses. I learned that when one starts the business instead of focussing on the amount of business or profits, more important is to reach to people, make your business reach to the people first by giving best of your services, no matter that service us profitable for you or not. It helps you develop the trust among the members and their networks.
    Thing I learned is to develop your PR, be sincere in your services.

    Reply

  40. Keith Besherse says

    June 26, 2019 at 11:00 pm

    BNI is “how” to network. Without the BNI model to use at Chamber of Commerce and Rotary, I would burn so many bridges without even knowing why.

    Reply

  41. Deanna Glaser says

    July 8, 2019 at 1:13 pm

    I am learning to concisely yet briefly describe my business and have learned how to present a specific “ask” that produces referrals.

    Reply

  42. Kelly Dobens says

    July 10, 2019 at 2:01 pm

    What’s working for me:
    Relationship (both professional and personal)
    Making me get outside my own box
    Improved public speaking
    Correcting my own business-person mistakes
    That’s just a few and I’m so happy that it was recommended to me to join.

    Reply

  43. Diane Lent says

    October 7, 2019 at 9:34 am

    Hi Ivan,

    On the “One thing that is working for me” pod cast, I would not be working in my dream career full time if not for BNI. I am a full time artist. I have always wanted to be a full time artist but believed from childhood this would not be possible. When I was working for another company as a BNI member I brought in one of my paintings and said my big hairy goal is to be a full time artist and I do this, showing my work. My BNI group said well, why not and I want one, I know people who would want one… So here I am 3 years later making a living as an artist in BNI.

    Reply

    • Herbert Wiggins says

      April 1, 2020 at 10:30 pm

      It’s all about referrals. Getting more and more. And I love giving them.

      Reply

    • Marsha Connell says

      August 24, 2021 at 1:36 am

      Diane, I’m delighted to hear of your success as an artist in BNI. It is a unique position which I share with you. I invite you to a zoom 1 2 1 to continue the conversation and to compare BNI experiences and strategies. Where are you? I am in BNI San Francisco Bay.

      Reply

  44. Priscilla N Myerson says

    January 4, 2021 at 5:32 pm

    Developing relationships with fellow members so that you know them on both a business and personal level.

    Reply

  45. MARIA LUZ JAVIER says

    April 20, 2021 at 7:33 pm

    I had good access to my suppliers was one of the good things I got from BNI.

    Reply

  46. Irene Bilinski says

    April 21, 2021 at 9:00 am

    BNI works for those that want it to work. If you leave a meeting and then forget completely about it till the next week, then it certainly will not work for you or very little. We all need to eat and have coffee, invite one member/week to have lunch or a coffee to get to know each other – its easier to help those that we know. My Chapter provides me with knowledgeable people who can help me and my clients with their services. I’ve been in the business for over 15 years and find no reason to not renew annually.

    Reply

  47. Rowena Finegan says

    August 18, 2021 at 11:35 pm

    Episode 600: one thing that’s working. I used to be horribly shy. As I got older, I got better, but I would still get nervous about speaking in public. Since I have been with BNI (1 year), I seem to haver overcome those fears to the point that I actually enjoy public speaking! Of, course, Zoom really helps. I am interested to see what happens once we are in person again!

    Reply

  48. Margarida Mateus says

    April 11, 2023 at 6:35 pm

    Para mim está a funcionar em várias frentes: alargar contactos, desenvolver foco e competências de comunicação e eu própria sinto-me a desenvolver a minha atitude de empresária e líder. Gratidão! Fantástico podcast.
    Forte abraço, a acreditar e em votos de sucessos e prosperidade partilhada.
    MM

    Reply

Products


  • Networking Is a Marathon, Not a Sprint     Learn how to Outlast, Outconnect, and Outperform the competition, through patience, consistency, and a giver’s mindset.

  • Infinite Legacy: The 7 Principles of Creating a Lasting Impact With Your Life

  • The 3rd Paradigm: A Radical Shift to Greater Success

  • Who's in Your Room? Revised and Updated: The Question That Will Change Your Life

  • Work Your Network With The 4Cs: Turn Every Handshake Into a Steady Stream of Referrals

  • The Connector Effect: The Proven Way to Grow Your Business Right Now

  • Infinite Giving: The 7 Principles of Givers Gain

  • The Networking Mentor: Whose Story Are You In?

  • Networking Like a Pro, 2nd Edition

  • World's Best-Known Marketing Secret

  • Healing Begins in the Kitchen: The Misner Plan

  • Avoiding the Networking Disconnect

  • 29% Solution     It's not "net-sit" or "net-eat," it's "net-WORK"

  • Business Networking and Sex (Not What You Think)     It's no surprise that communicating with the opposite sex can be tricky. Finally, help is here.

  • Masters of Success     Proven Techniques for Achieving Success in Business and Life

  • Truth or Delusion?     Busting Networking's Biggest Myths
